description: Expanded short humorous shopkeeper and fisherman dialogue lines for Patient Angler, including player-name personalization and gameplay tips.

normalGreetings: Fresh deals! Slightly fishy prices.
Welcome back, coin collector.
Need gear or just admiring my shelves?
Everything here is waterproof. Mostly.
Step right up! No refunds if it bites.
I polished the rods. The fish remain unimpressed.
Buy now before the crabs unionize.
I sell quality gear and questionable advice.
Looking for upgrades or emotional support?
The bait is fresh. The jokes are not.
Welcome! Try not to spend responsibly.
Coins in, fishing empire out.
I had a sale yesterday. You missed it. Maybe.
New stock! Same smell.
If it breaks, blame the lake.
I accept coins, diamonds, and compliments.
One rod upgrade away from greatness.
The fish asked me not to sell you this.
Careful, these prices are almost legal.
Welcome to the finest shop near this puddle.
I found these upgrades floating downstream.
Spend wisely. Or loudly. I enjoy both.
Every great angler starts by overpaying.
This shop is 90% wood and 10% ambition.
The nets are trained. The boats are moody.
I tested the bait myself. Bad idea.
Need something shiny for your fishing problem?
My gear catches fish. Eventually.
Step inside! The smell means quality.
Business is booming. Unlike that old naval mine.
Storage full again? That is a profitable problem.
A better rod means fewer taps and fewer regrets.
Automation never complains about overtime.
Targeted Lure helps when one Fishdex entry refuses to cooperate.
Premium Bait is expensive because the fish have standards.
Black Pearls work best when you decide whether to hoard or spend.
Prestige hurts briefly. The pearls remember forever.
Old zones keep producing. Never abandon a good pond.
Fishdex Mastery rewards patience more than luck.
Trophy fish are rare. Bragging about them is mandatory.
A full storage is just unsold ambition.
The market pays for fish, not excuses.
Upgrade automation before blaming the fish.
Targeted Lure increases weight, not certainty. Fish remain stubborn.
Competition prizes scale with your automation. Build first, boast later.
Seagulls pay better when your empire earns better.
Manual fishing is still the best way to find trophies.
Special fish follow the clock, not your schedule.
Some fish only appear when sensible anglers are asleep.
The Fishdex never forgets. Unlike me.
You can spend pearls on power or keep them for sell value.
The best upgrade is the one you can still afford afterward.
Offline progress stops eventually. Even fishermen need sleep.
A new zone is expensive because the fish demanded better scenery.
Rod tiers look harmless until the price gains extra zeroes.
Mastery bonuses are permanent. Your patience has receipts.
Sell before storage fills, unless you enjoy wasted catches.
Check Statistics when the multipliers start looking suspicious.
Competitions are short. The excuses can be shorter.
Grand Winners are made over ten competitions, not one lucky catch.
{playerName}, my shelves missed your coins.
Good to see you, {playerName}. The fish asked if you retired.
{playerName}, I saved the expensive items just for you.
Welcome, {playerName}. Let us pretend this is your last upgrade.
The fish are nervous again, {playerName}. You must be shopping.
{playerName}, your {rodName} is doing fine. Fine can always be improved.
Back in {zone}, {playerName}? Then buy like the locals are watching.
{playerName}, {fishCaught} catches is impressive. The smell is less so.
{playerName}, {blackPearls} Black Pearls can buy power or patience.
Prestige number {prestigeCount}, {playerName}? You really enjoy starting over.

quickReturnGreetings.message: Back already? Did the door hit you twice?
That was fast. Forgot to buy half the shop?
You barely left. The dust has not settled.
Fastest fishing trip ever. Catch anything?
{playerName}, you forgot something... or missed my face.

longAbsenceGreetings.messageTemplate: Gone for {timeAway}? I was starting to blame a sea monster.
{timeAway} away! I almost sold your usual bait to a gull.
After {timeAway}, you return! The fish filed a missing angler report.
You vanished for {timeAway}. I kept the shop open out of spite.
{timeAway} without shopping? Your coins must have been terrified.
{playerName}! Gone for {timeAway}. I almost printed missing posters.
After {timeAway}, {playerName} returns. The sea monster must have lost.
{playerName}, {timeAway} is a long fishing trip. At least bring a trophy.

tips: Tip: Old zones keep producing after you unlock new ones.
Tip: Targeted Lure increases selected loot weight, but does not guarantee it.
Tip: Manual fishing is required for legendary and special time-window fish.
Tip: Trophy fish can only be fully claimed through manual fishing.
Tip: Black Pearls give passive sell value while unspent.
Tip: Pearl Shop upgrades are permanent through Prestige.
Tip: Fishdex Mastery progress is permanent through Prestige.
Tip: Prestige resets your economy, not your long-term knowledge.
Tip: Storage overflow can waste catches, so upgrade before it becomes a habit.
Tip: Competition rewards scale from your estimated automation income.
Tip: Ten competitions form one series with Grand Winners at the end.
Tip: Seagull rewards scale with your automation income.
Tip: Premium Bait improves rarity weights for a limited time.
Tip: Special fish only appear during their listed in-game time window.
Tip: Rod tiers improve permanent bonuses but become increasingly expensive.
Tip: Statistics Tab 8 explains where your active bonuses come from.
Tip: Targeted Lure slots increase as the lure is upgraded.
Tip: Offline progress has a cap, so checking in occasionally still matters.
Tip: Automation Fishdex and Manual Fishdex track different collections.
Tip: Overall Fishdex completion includes every unique fish, plant, and trash item.

implementationNotes.selectionRule: When opening shop: if time since last shop visit < 10 seconds, randomly use quickReturnGreetings. Else if >= 5 real hours, randomly use longAbsenceGreetings and replace placeholders. Otherwise randomly use normalGreetings. Optionally use a tip instead of a greeting at a low rate such as 15%, but never show both at once.

globalRules.frequency: At most once per in-game hour per visible fishing screen.
globalRules.style: Short, humorous one-liners. Some lines may include player name or lightweight gameplay tips.

river: River fish are slippery. River socks are worse.
I asked the current for advice. It kept moving.
If you catch my lunch, please negotiate nicely.
This river knows secrets. Mostly where I dropped my hook.
I saw a boot swim by. Very suspicious.
The fish here respect patience and fear bread.
A calm river hides dramatic fish.
I named that ripple Kevin.
If the line pulls hard, pull harder. Unless it's a log.
The river paid me in splashes again.
Nets work slowly, but they never ask for lunch.
River automation keeps working even after you leave this zone.
A Targeted Lure helps when one species keeps hiding.
The Fishdex notices every unique catch. It is very judgmental.
Special fish obey the clock more than the angler.
Manual fishing is still your best chance at a trophy.
Upgrade storage before the river fills it for you.
A full net is good. A full storage is complicated.
River Rod tiers make Nets faster. The nets appreciate recognition.
Competition rewards like a strong automation empire.
That fish looked rare. Or expensive. Same thing, usually.
The current changes. My fishing spot does not.
Somewhere upstream, a fish is blaming you.
I lost a hook here once. It may be running the place now.
The river is free. The upgrades are not.
{playerName}, the river says hello. I think.
Try not to scare every fish today, {playerName}.
I have seen worse anglers than you, {playerName}. Not many.
{playerName}, your {rodName} looks less tired than mine.
{fishCaught} catches, {playerName}? The river remembers.
Tip: River Rod tiers improve Net automation speed.
Tip: Old-zone automation keeps producing after you move on.
Tip: Time-window fish require the correct in-game hour.
Tip: Targeted Lure affects automation catches only.
Tip: Manual Trophy catches count toward records and competitions.
lake: The lake is calm. Too calm. Probably plotting.
I once fought a carp for three minutes. We are friends now.
Big fish live deep. My snacks do too.
If you hear bubbles, pretend it's wisdom.
The lake keeps everything. Especially bicycles.
I trust the bobber more than most people.
Some say there is a monster here. I say it owes me bait.
Quiet day. Loud fish.
The catfish blinked first. I think.
Never underestimate a fish with free time.
Fishermen automation improves with Lake Rod tiers.
The lake rewards patience and forgets nothing.
Targeted Lure is useful when the Fishdex has one stubborn gap.
Manual fish hide behind time windows and bad sleep schedules.
Trophy fish do not care how much automation you own.
Storage fills quietly. Then suddenly.
If the bobber moves, react. If I move, bring snacks.
A rare catch is luck. Repeating it is mastery.
Fishdex Mastery survives Prestige. Knowledge floats.
The deepest part of the lake is where my confidence went.
That bicycle has been down there longer than I have.
The fish are biting. My jokes are not.
A better rod means fewer taps and more dignity.
I caught nothing all morning. Very peaceful.
The lake is generous when it feels watched.
{playerName}, today feels like a legendary day. Or rain.
Even the lake knows your name now, {playerName}.
Do not let the fish outsmart you, {playerName}. Again.
{playerName}, your {rodName} has better posture than mine.
{highestZone} already, {playerName}? The lake feels abandoned.
Tip: Lake Rod tiers improve Fisherman automation speed.
Tip: Manual Fishdex entries do not count toward automation mastery.
Tip: Fishdex Mastery bonuses are permanent through Prestige.
Tip: Trophy fish must be caught manually for full rewards.
Tip: Check storage before long offline periods.
bay: The bay smells like profit and questionable seaweed.
Saltwater fish act fancy until they meet a net.
I waved at a crab. It filed a complaint.
If the tide takes your bait, charge it rent.
Bay fishing: half skill, half arguing with waves.
A gull judged my technique. Fair, honestly.
Somewhere out there, a fish is making bad decisions.
I caught a rope once. Strong personality.
The wind says buy better gear. Convenient.
The bay gives, the bay takes, the bay splashes your boots.
Bay Rod tiers improve storage. The fish dislike organized anglers.
Boats catch faster than fishermen and complain less.
The tide rises. So do upgrade prices.
A full hold is profit waiting for a button press.
Competition series are won by consistency, not one lucky fish.
Grand Winners get remembered. Fourth place gets character.
Seagulls know exactly when your hands are busy.
The market loves fish and ignores emotional attachment.
Premium Bait makes rare catches less shy for a while.
Targeted Lure cannot target special manual-only fish.
That crab has been watching us for twenty minutes.
The bay keeps secrets badly. Everything washes ashore.
A boat is just a floating coin problem.
The wind changed direction. My plan did not.
Some fish are valuable. Some are educational trash.
The tide favors you today, {playerName}. Do not waste it.
{playerName}, even the seagulls are taking notes.
Let us see if the bay feels generous, {playerName}.
{playerName}, your {rodName} looks expensive from here.
{blackPearls} Black Pearls, {playerName}? The shopkeeper is smiling.
Tip: Bay Rod tiers increase storage capacity.
Tip: Boat automation speed can be improved by rod tiers and permanent bonuses.
Tip: Grand Winners are decided after ten competitions.
Tip: Seagull rewards scale with estimated hourly automation income.
Tip: Premium Bait changes rarity weights, not guaranteed outcomes.
